description: GET /v1/realtime with WS_TRANSPORT_ENABLED=false. Returns 503, never 403 — this is a transport/availability fact, not an authorization failure; the same bearer token would be accepted by every other /v1 route. Matches v1-architecture.md §8.1 and openapi.yaml #/components/responses/TransportUnavailable.

paired_snapshot_state.description: Immediately before/after this call, GET /v1/snapshot with the same token returns capabilities.ws_transport_enabled: false and still fully serves space state — it is not affected by the switch.

notes: A client seeing 503 should fall back to POST /v1/events + polling GET /v1/snapshot, not treat its credential as bad.

POST /v1/automations and POST /v1/events are unaffected by this switch — v1-architecture.md §8.1 explicitly removes the old /v3/automations realtimeDisabled() 403 gate; only the WS upgrade itself is gated.
